summary.intent: Merge a feature branch (summary-budget-discipline) into main, then run a Greptile review loop on PR #652 to fix any code quality issues before finalizing.
summary.outcome: Merge completed with 3 conflict resolutions, Greptile review triggered and completed with success, all 3 inline comments addressed (off-by-one clarity, omission notice undercount, blank line stripping), 19 tests passing, fixes committed and pushed.

repo: compress_summary_in_history() is a post-processing step applied to compacted history that finds summary-prefixed messages ([Conversation Summary] / [Conversation Compacted]) and compresses only the body, preserving head/tail structure for modes like recency_preserving.
Merge conflicts between this branch and main were isolated to crates/chat/src/lib.rs in three areas: compaction logging, run_compaction refactor, and tests.
Greptile threads are auto-resolved after fixes are pushed — no manual resolution needed.

code.finding: Off-by-one in summary compression already fixed via .saturating_sub(1) in commit 8fd89861; clarity improvement applied.

Pre-computed char_budget uses make_notice(all_dropped_count) before the header drop loop runs, so if headers are also dropped the notice grows and causes a tiny budget overshoot. Fixed by recomputing budget inside the header loop.
Blank lines in summary bodies each consume one character slot. Stripping them saves budget; HashSet dedup already handled duplicate blanks but did not eliminate them.

workflow: Greptile review must be manually triggered via API after a push; it does not auto-trigger on every push.
Polling Greptile check status with jq can fail on unexpected response shapes; a Python fallback is more robust.
Running cargo test scoped to the relevant test module (compress_summary) is faster than a full test suite run for iterative fix verification.

friction: Greptile polling script produced jq parse errors requiring a stop and restart with a Python-based fallback.
Greptile review body was empty after completion, requiring a separate fetch of inline comments to get actionable feedback.
Confidence score was not easily surfaced — required an additional API call to the check run details.
open_items: The omission notice undercount fix (recomputing budget inside header drop loop) is a correctness fix for a tiny edge case — worth verifying under adversarial inputs with very long headers.
New Greptile review after the fix commit was triggered but results not shown in transcript — follow-up may be needed to confirm no new issues were introduced.
